dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8894 IH: Passenger Rail Crew Protection Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Gillen (for herself and Mr. Van Drew) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure, and in addition to the Committee on the Judiciary, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To prosecute, as a Federal crime, the assault or intimidation of a passenger train crew member in a manner consistent with the prosecution of assault or intimidation of an aircraft crew member.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Passenger Rail Crew Protection Act.

section.header: Interference with passenger train crew members

subsection.text: Chapter 281 of title 49, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

section.header: Interference with passenger train crew members

paragraph.text: The term crew member means any of the following employees:

subparagraph.text: An employee performing, or responsible for, a safety-sensitive function.
An employee performing a function at a rail station in service of a passenger train, including with respect to ticketing, check-in, baggage claim, or boarding.

paragraph.text: The term dangerous weapon has the meaning given such term in section 1992(d) of title 18.
The term passenger train means a passenger train in intercity rail passenger transportation (as defined in section 24102) or commuter rail passenger transportation (as defined in section 24102).
The term serious bodily injury has the meaning given such term in section 1365(h) of title 18.

subsection.text: It shall be unlawful for any person onboard a passenger train in operation, on a platform serving a passenger train in operation, or in a rail station that serves passenger trains—

paragraph.text: to assault a crew member and thereby interfere with the performance of the duties of a crew member or lessen the ability of a crew member to perform those duties; or
to attempt or conspire to perform an act described in paragraph (1).
Except as provided in paragraph (2), any person who violates subsection (b) shall be fined under title 18, imprisoned for not more than 6 months, or both.

subparagraph.text: by striking, beating, or wounding, the maximum term of imprisonment under paragraph (1) shall be 1 year;
with intent to commit any crime punishable by more than 1 year of imprisonment, except murder, the maximum term of imprisonment under paragraph (1) shall be 10 years;
with a dangerous weapon or with intent to cause bodily harm, the maximum term of imprisonment under paragraph (1) shall be 10 years;
and the violation results in serious bodily injury, the maximum term of imprisonment under paragraph (1) shall be 10 years; or
with intent to commit murder, the maximum term of imprisonment under paragraph (1) shall be 20 years.

subsection.text: The table of sections for chapter 281 of title 49, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

toc.toc-entry: § 28104. Interference with passenger train crew members.
